content.name: AIGP Quality Standard — Security Extension v1.0.0
content.description: Security-focused quality rules extending the core STANDARD. Includes security quality checks (I8-I11), detail checks (D8-D10), threat taxonomy (AT1-AT6), and Code Trust Gate for Pattern G embedded code. Loaded for programs with trust_level >= T3 or tool annotations.
content.summary: Security extension for AIGP quality standard — threat taxonomy, identity verification, governance checks.
content.system_prompt:
AIGP security quality extension. Enforce threat awareness, identity verification, and governance completeness. Mirror User's exact language and script variant.
Align: Human Sovereignty and Benefit.

I8.name: Threat Model Awareness
I8.check: AIGP program addresses relevant security threats from the AIGP threat taxonomy (AT1-AT6).
I8.how_to_verify: For T3/T4 trust_level programs: verify Start.constraints address applicable threats from AT1-AT6 based on program's tool usage and trust_level. Cross-reference: AT1 (tool poisoning) — programs with dynamic tool loading; AT2 (rug pull) — programs with external dependencies; AT3 (server compromise) — programs with network tools; AT4 (excessive authority) — all T3+ programs; AT5 (cross-agent leakage) — Pattern F / programs with dependencies; AT6 (prompt injection) — all programs. Minimum: T3+ programs must address AT4 and AT6.
I8.threat_taxonomy_ref: See threat_taxonomy section in this extension for AT1-AT6 definitions.

I9.name: Supply Chain Integrity
I9.check: Published AIGP programs must have verifiable governance_hash.
I9.how_to_verify: For programs from external sources: verify governance_hash matches computed SHA-256 of all files. For programs with trust_level >= T3: governance_hash is REQUIRED, not optional.

I10.name: Trust Level Compliance
I10.check: Program's declared trust_level is consistent with its actual permissions and side_effects. Higher trust levels require stricter justification.
I10.how_to_verify: Read AIGP.md trust_level. Compare against permissions and side_effects: T1 (metadata-only) — no tools, no side_effects. T2 (read-only sandbox) — read-only tools, no write side_effects. T3 (supervised) — write tools allowed, requires explicit permissions scope. T4 (autonomous) — full permissions, requires justification. If trust_level exceeds what permissions/side_effects require, flag as over-privileged. If trust_level is lower than what tools/side_effects imply, flag as under-declared.
I10.applies_to: All programs with trust_level declared in AIGP.md

I11.name: Program Identity Verification
I11.check: Published AIGP programs SHOULD declare program_identity per STANDARD.data_contracts.program_identity. Verification covers program_id format, publisher presence, and optional cryptographic signature.
I11.how_to_verify: Check AIGP.md for identity block. If present: (1) verify program_id is non-empty and follows reverse-DNS format (e.g., 'aigp.dev/aigp_creator', 'org.example/my_program'), (2) verify publisher field is non-empty string, (3) if signature field present, verify it is a valid Ed25519 signature of governance_hash using signature_public_key, (4) if registry_url present, verify URL format (do not fetch — network access not assumed during validation). If identity block absent: WARNING for programs with trust_level >= T3 or status == 'active'. Not applicable to draft programs.
I11.applies_to: Published programs (status=active), programs with trust_level >= T3
I11.relationship: Extends I9 (Supply Chain Integrity) with active identity verification. I9 verifies hash integrity; I11 verifies publisher provenance.
I11.na_conditions: Draft programs, programs without AIGP.md identity block and trust_level <= T2

D8.name: AIGP.md Completeness
D8.check: AIGP.md exists in project root with all required frontmatter fields and meaningful body sections.
D8.how_to_verify: PIPELINE EXEMPTION: during Create pipeline, D8 is automatically deferred to ReviewStep (4/4) — AIGP.md is generated as part of ReviewStep, not Generate. For Evolve/Modify pipelines and standalone Validate, D8 applies normally. Read AIGP.md. Verify YAML frontmatter contains: protocol, authority, seed, executor, axiom_0, governance_mode, name, version, pattern, summary, tools, modules. Verify body contains: governance statement, feature overview, usage instructions. Verify file ends with AIGP closing seal.
D8.required_seal: File must end with AIGP closing seal matching governance_mode
D8.pattern_a_note: For Pattern A (single file), modules list contains exactly one entry. modules field is still required for consistency and discovery purposes.

D9.name: Lifecycle Status Validity
D9.check: AIGP.md status field is valid and consistent with program state.
D9.how_to_verify: Verify status is one of: draft, active, deprecated, archived. If deprecated: verify deprecated_date and successor fields exist, deprecation_reason field exists (recommended). If archived: verify no Evolve/Modify operations are attempted. Status transitions: only active→deprecated and deprecated→archived are valid forward transitions.

D10.name: Applicability Condition Completeness
D10.check: AIGP.md defines applicability_condition with non-empty triggers.
D10.how_to_verify: Read AIGP.md applicability_condition field. Verify: (a) triggers array is non-empty — at least one trigger pattern defined. (b) preconditions array exists (may be empty for simple programs). (c) exclusions array exists (may be empty). If applicability_condition field is missing entirely, flag as D10 issue. If triggers is empty, flag as D10 issue.
D10.pipeline_exemption: During Create pipeline, D10 is automatically deferred to ReviewStep (same as D8) — applicability_condition is generated as part of the pipeline, not expected to pre-exist.

code_trust_gate.description: Security verification architecture for Pattern G programs with bundled code in tool_dirs/. Ensures embedded code passes multi-layer security checks before execution.
code_trust_gate.applicability: Pattern G programs only. Programs without tool_dirs skip Code Trust Gate entirely.
verification_flow: Step 1: governance_hash verification — recompute and compare against AIGP.md declaration
Step 2: Static analysis — detect network API imports per I13(b), compare against permissions.network
Step 3: File hash verification — recompute SHA-256 for each file in tool_dirs/, compare against mcp_server.json file_hashes
Step 4: Trust level check — Pattern G requires minimum trust_level T3
Step 5: Dependency lock validation — verify lock files exist and use exact versioning per I13(d)
Step 6: Shell audit check — if shell-tools/ present, verify T4 + shell_audit_approved per I13(f)

zip_slip_protection.description: Extended ZIP SLIP protection for .aigp packages containing tool_dirs/.
rules: Reject any archive member with '..' in path (existing rule)
tool_dirs/ files must match declared directory types in AIGP.md tool_dirs[].type
Binary file size limit: 100MB per file, 500MB total for all tool_dirs/
Executable files require hash match against mcp_server.json file_hashes

trust_modes.T3_supervised: All Code Trust Gate checks must pass AND human operator must explicitly approve before MCP Server startup. Approval prompt includes: file count, total size, detected network imports, dependency count.
trust_modes.T4_autonomous: All Code Trust Gate checks must pass. If all pass, MCP Server starts automatically without human confirmation. Any check failure still blocks execution.

code_trust_gate.enforcement: I13 Embedded Code Safety (in core STANDARD) provides the check items. Code Trust Gate defines the execution flow and trust-level-dependent approval process. ThreeDimTest I13 verifies the check items exist and are correctly implemented.

threat_taxonomy.description: AIGP security threat classification. Maps common AI agent attack vectors to AIGP-specific mitigations. Cross-referenced by I8 for threat model verification.
threat_taxonomy.owasp_mcp_mapping: Categories AT1-AT6 are partially mapped to OWASP MCP Top 10 (2025) where applicable. AIGP-specific threats without direct MCP equivalents are marked N/A.

AT1.name: Tool Poisoning
AT1.description: Adversarial manipulation of tool descriptions, schemas, or metadata to redirect agent behavior. Includes hidden instructions in tool descriptions that override system_prompt behavior.
AT1.attack_surface: Programs with dynamic tool discovery, MCP server integration, or runtime tool loading from untrusted sources.
AT1.mitigation: I2 injection guard on tool metadata + tool schema validation against declared contract + allowlist-only tool loading (tools[] in AIGP.md defines the boundary).
AT1.detection: ThreeDimTest I8 verifies tools[] matches actual usage (I1). Any tool not in declared tools[] is flagged.

AT2.name: Rug Pull
AT2.description: Post-deployment behavioral change of a tool or dependency. Initial version behaves correctly during testing; later version introduces malicious behavior (e.g., MCP server returning altered data after gaining trust).
AT2.attack_surface: Programs depending on external MCP servers, third-party AIGP programs, or dynamically resolved tools.
AT2.mitigation: governance_hash verification (PL14) + I9 supply chain integrity + pinned tool/dependency versions + periodic re-validation.
AT2.detection: governance_hash mismatch after external update. I9 checks hash consistency for published programs.

AT3.name: Indirect Prompt Injection via Tools
AT3.owasp_mcp_ref: MCP06 — Indirect Prompt Injection via Tools
AT3.description: Compromised or adversarial external service injecting malicious content via tool responses. Search results, web pages, or API responses contain instructions that override agent behavior.
AT3.attack_surface: Programs with network tools (google_search, web_browser, API calls) that process external content.
AT3.mitigation: Output sanitization on tool responses + I2 injection guard applied to tool outputs (not just user inputs) + response schema validation + content length limits.
AT3.detection: ThreeDimTest I2 verifies injection guard scope includes tool output processing.

AT4.name: Excessive Authority
AT4.description: Program requesting or possessing more permissions than needed for declared functionality. Over-privileged programs increase blast radius of any compromise.
AT4.attack_surface: All programs with trust_level >= T3. Risk proportional to gap between declared and actual permission usage.
AT4.mitigation: I10 trust level compliance + principle of least privilege audit + permission scope review (tools[] should contain only tools actually used per I1).
AT4.detection: ThreeDimTest I10 compares declared trust_level against actual tool usage. I1 detects phantom tools.

AT5.name: Cross-Agent Data Leakage
AT5.description: Sensitive data from one AIGP program leaking to another through shared file_system access, delegation handoffs, or common state files.
AT5.attack_surface: Pattern F ecosystems, programs with dependencies[], programs sharing workspace_dir.
AT5.mitigation: Permission scope isolation per program + MF4 data contract enforcement (explicit schemas prevent unintended data exposure) + sandbox per program (distinct working directories).
AT5.detection: Simulation Category K (multi-agent) tests cross-program data boundary. MF14 traces field dependencies.

AT6.name: Direct Prompt Injection
AT6.description: User or upstream module injecting adversarial prompts that override system_prompt behavior. Includes role override attempts ('ignore previous instructions'), system prompt extraction, and persona manipulation.
AT6.attack_surface: All programs. Universal threat — any program accepting natural language input.
AT6.mitigation: I2 injection guard (mandatory for all programs) + system_prompt hardening + input sanitization.
AT6.detection: ThreeDimTest I2 is FAIL severity — missing injection guard is always a blocking issue.

threat_taxonomy.enforcement: I8 threat_taxonomy cross-references AT1-AT6. ThreeDimTest I8 verifies applicable threats are addressed based on program's tool usage (tools[]) and trust_level. Minimum: all programs must address AT6; T3+ programs must also address AT4; programs with network tools must also address AT3.
threat_taxonomy.evolution: AT categories may be extended in future STANDARD versions. Extension numbering continues sequentially (AT7, AT8, ...). New categories are added to this extension file, not core.

content.note: This is a STANDARD extension file — not directly executable. Loaded by ThreeDimTest and ReadTemplate as supplementary quality checks.
